title: Assevra: A Reliability Scorecard for LLM Agents
description: Assevra is an open-source toolkit and methodology for measuring the reliability of large language model (LLM) agents. It scores an agent on four dimensions - grounding, safety/refusal, PII leakage, and task completion - each against a fixed threshold with a 95% Wilson confidence interval, and issues a reproducible reliability scorecard as Markdown, JSON, and a self-contained HTML report. Deterministic dimensions run offline; LLM-as-a-judge dimensions use a pinned model and are skipped, not failed, when unavailable. This is the reference implementation of the Assevra Reliability Scorecard methodology.
